she reaches for the door handle but doesn't turn it yet
the glass falls from his hand and shatters on the tile
she turns around slowly, having heard her name
he stands up from the chair for the first time that morning
the letter catches in the draft and slides across the floor
she lowers the phone without answering, eyes still on the window
he takes one step forward and then stops
the curtain falls back into place behind him
she sets the cup down without drinking
he looks at the empty passenger seat, then drives off
she places the ring on the table and walks out of frame
he turns his face into the light and closes his eyes
the train passes through, she doesn't board
she opens the envelope but doesn't read it yet
